officialSources.title: Web Integration Guidance
borrowedRules: NHS App webview adds nhsapp-android or nhsapp-ios markers to the user agent
query parameter recognition such as from=nhsApp is guidance for styling, errors, and invalid sessions
NHS App SSO redirects to base URL plus journey path with assertedLoginIdentity query parameter
NHS login authorize must use prompt=none
ConsentNotGiven must return to a suitable message with a link back to the NHS App
site links need coordination with the NHS App onboarding team and platform-specific association files

officialSources.title: Javascript API v2 Specification
borrowedRules: new integrations should use JS API v2 and load the script inline
cached JS API files can persist up to 1 year and newer functionality should use a query suffix
available functions include isOpenInNHSApp, setBackAction, clearBackAction, goToHomePage, goToPage, openBrowserOverlay, openExternalBrowser, addEventToCalendar, and downloadFromBytes
getAppPlatform and isOpenInNHSApp are based on user-agent detection and are treated as evidence only in this pack

officialSources.title: NHS App web integration
borrowedRules: web integrations surface a responsive website in the NHS App
the integration process includes expression of interest, product assessment, Sandpit, AOS, SCAL, connection agreement, limited release, full release, and post-live obligations
headers and footers may need changes to fit the NHS App integration
significant journey changes require product assessment lead time

officialSources.title: Standards for NHS App integration
borrowedRules: NHS App integrations must meet WCAG 2.2 AA
service standard, clinical safety, and data privacy evidence shape integration readiness

officialSources.title: Accessibility guidance for testing
borrowedRules: embedded shell recovery terminology must remain testable by keyboard, focus, labels, headings, zoom, and assistive announcement checks
manual and automated accessibility checks remain required evidence inputs for later Phase 7 guardrails

acceptedInto375: user-agent and JS API detection are evidence only, not authority
query hints are styling and recovery hints only
assertedLoginIdentity is immediately captured, hashed, redacted, and converted to asserted_login_identity for NHS login
prompt=none and ConsentNotGiven outcomes are first-class SSO return dispositions
JS API v2 capabilities are represented as BridgeCapabilityMatrix entries and route-scoped BridgeActionLease rules
site links are launch metadata only and do not replace AccessGrantService or RouteIntentBinding

rejectedOrDeferred.claim: from=nhsApp query parameter proves trusted embedded mode
rejectedOrDeferred.reason: official guidance frames query parameters as recognition/styling guidance; local blueprint requires signed evidence or validated SSO for trust

rejectedOrDeferred.claim: isOpenInNHSApp is enough to unlock bridge actions
rejectedOrDeferred.reason: JS API detection is user-agent based, so bridge actions still require PatientEmbeddedNavEligibility

rejectedOrDeferred.claim: assertedLoginIdentity can stay in the URL until frontend boot
rejectedOrDeferred.reason: the token is query-borne and must be redacted before logging, analytics, history, or referrer exposure

rejectedOrDeferred.claim: site-link setup can create a second resume model
rejectedOrDeferred.reason: local Phase 0 keeps grant issuance, redemption, revocation, and route-intent authority in AccessGrantService and RouteIntentBinding

rejectedOrDeferred.claim: file download behavior should be solved in this pack
rejectedOrDeferred.reason: this pack freezes bridge capability and navigation leases; artifact byte delivery and degraded file behavior are owned by 376

openExternalDependencies: NHS login SSO integration details and environment-specific redirect configuration remain implementation inputs for par_379
NHS App team-provided Android package names, certificate fingerprints, iOS app IDs, and environment values remain future onboarding inputs
NHS App JS API production, Sandpit, and AOS environment URLs must be supplied by the runtime environment registry before live use
